summary: Problem 10 is an algorithm-design and complexity-analysis task on preconditioned CG for RKHS-constrained CP decomposition of tensors with missing entries, scored at 57% solvability and judged tractable since its core ingredients (CG, Khatri-Rao/Kronecker matvec tricks) are standard. However, the current state has no evaluable proof: the sole tracked issue was a seed/placeholder that was closed without any substantive proof being submitted, and the 'best proof' record paradoxically shows 100 open issues with no concrete artifact.
problems: No actual proof or algorithm derivation exists yet — the only issue was a placeholder seed tracker closed by the system with 'no proof to evaluate'.
Metadata inconsistency: 'Best Proof' status reports 100 open issues and an unknown experiment, while the issue list shows only 1 resolved issue, making the true progress state unclear.

Key mathematical components are unspecified: the convergence/conditioning analysis of the preconditioner, the handling of missing entries (masked least squares), and the RKHS constraint formulation are not yet written down.
Without a stated objective, KKT/normal-equation system, and preconditioner definition, the complexity claims (e.g., per-iteration matvec cost via Khatri-Rao structure) cannot be verified.
highlights: The problem is well-scoped and rated tractable (57%) as an algorithm-design rather than deep theorem-proving task.
Authors are domain experts (Kolda, Ward) and the required building blocks — conjugate gradient, Kronecker/Khatri-Rao fast matvecs — are mature and well-documented.
The original placeholder issue is correctly resolved, leaving a clean slate for a focused first proof draft.

suggested_todos.title: Reconcile metadata: determine whether 100 open issues exist or only the one resolved placeholder, and establish the true baseline
Draft the formal problem setup: masked CP objective with missing entries, RKHS smoothness constraint, and the resulting normal-equations/KKT linear system
Specify the preconditioner explicitly and prove a conditioning/convergence bound for CG on the RKHS-constrained subproblem
Derive per-iteration matvec cost using Khatri-Rao/Kronecker structure and state the overall complexity claim with proof
Open targeted issues for each subproof (missing-entry masking correctness, preconditioner SPD-ness, convergence rate) instead of a single seed tracker
Run a small numerical sanity check on a synthetic unaligned tensor to validate the algorithm before finalizing the analysis
